title: Faramea papirifolia
intro: Faramea papirifolia, also known as "Papery Faramea," is a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, as well as potential treatments for fever, digestive issues, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Faramea papirifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Faramea papirifolia is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to leverage its health benefits, with the leaves being the primary parts used for medicinal purposes.

This family consists of about 600 genera and over 13,000 species, and is one of the largest families of flowering plants.
